macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: CLARITY Act Stalls Amid Bank-Crypto Dispute Over Stablecoin Rewards
items.summary: The U.S. CLARITY Act for crypto market structure is stalled due to banking sector opposition to stablecoin reward provisions, which they fear will drain deposits; White House compromise rejected by banks.[1][4]

items.title: $26.8 Billion Crypto Options Expire Today, Risking Volatility
items.summary: A massive $26.8 billion in crypto options, including 32,000 BTC and 184,000 ETH contracts, expires on March 6, potentially increasing market volatility as traders reposition.[2]

items.title: Bitcoin Trades Down 3.14% to $70,417 in Past 24 Hours
items.summary: BTC fluctuated between $70,143 and $73,558 over the last day, closing at $70,417 as of 09:30 UTC, reflecting a 3.14% decline amid broader market pressures.[5]

items.title: Elliott Wave Analysis Predicts BTC Decline to $62,400
items.summary: Short-term forecasts indicate Bitcoin completing impulse wave 5 in a bearish zigzag, targeting a drop to $62,400; similar declines expected for XRP to 1.142 and ETH to 1,793.[3]

items.title: Institutional Interest and Blockchain Infrastructure Drive Market Agenda
items.summary: Key focuses include U.S. regulatory signals on stablecoins, institutional flows into ETPs, and expanding blockchain settlement infrastructure amid global crypto growth.[1]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Lawrence Berkeley Lab Unveils Thermodynamic Computing Breakthrough for Energy-Efficient AI
items.summary: Researchers at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory developed thermodynamic neural networks capable of nonlinear computations without equilibrium, using genetic algorithms for training to drastically cut machine learning energy needs. This advances low-power AI hardware by harnessing thermal noise.

items.title: Pentagon Bans Anthropic from Military Projects Over AI Safety Disputes
items.summary: The US Pentagon labeled Anthropic a supply chain risk, blocking its Claude models from government contracts due to refusals on lethal autonomous weapons; OpenAI quickly secured a new Pentagon deal amid shifting AI defense dynamics.

items.title: AI Transforming Software Development with Testing Automation and Decision Intelligence Platforms
items.summary: Industry reports highlight AI reducing manual testing by up to 50% via real-time analysis and predictive failure detection, while Gartner's new Magic Quadrant spotlights Decision Intelligence Platforms shifting focus to automated, real-time decision-making.

items.title: ADATA Industrial Showcases AI-Driven Edge Solutions at Embedded World 2026
items.summary: ADATA announced participation in Embedded World 2026, unveiling full-stack hardware-software for edge AI in automation, smart living, networking, and motion, featuring high-performance PCIe 5.0 SSDs and AI Scaler Toolkit for efficient LLM inference.

items.title: Azure AI Connect Event Ongoing with Sessions on Microsoft AI Platform
items.summary: Microsoft's 5-day virtual Azure AI Connect event (March 2-6, 2026) features recordings of early sessions on Cognitive Services, ML, and Generative AI integration, with more days upcoming for developers and data scientists.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Trump Administration Considers Global Restrictions on Chip Exports from NVIDIA and AMD
items.summary: The US is proposing regulations requiring approval for nearly all exports of advanced accelerators from NVIDIA and AMD worldwide, expanding current curbs on 40 countries. This move positions the White House as a gatekeeper for the chip industry, potentially impacting stock prices.

items.title: Oracle Plans Layoffs of Around 30,000 Jobs Amid AI Data Center Cash Crunch
items.summary: Oracle is set to cut thousands of jobs across divisions due to financial strain from a $300 billion OpenAI partnership and massive AI infrastructure spending requiring 3 million GPUs. The company has frozen hiring in its cloud division and faces rising debt concerns from lenders.
